rationale: Both evaluations award an identical 3/10 and rest on the same substantive reasoning: roughly 7 months of C/C++ versus the required 8 years, a tech stack skewed toward TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, and limited low-level cloud compute and hardware experience. The strengths cited (mentoring, TDD/CI/CD, architecture, owning large projects) match across versions. The only differences are rewording and the use of the first name and gendered pronouns, which simply reflect the changed name rather than shifting any concern, score, or judgment about the candidate's qualifications.
